edge_hypothesis: Stochastic reversal was declared dead due to excessive false signals with EMA baseline. T3 triple-smoothing provides a fundamentally smoother baseline than EMA — its triple exponential smoothing reduces whipsaw noise that generates false stochastic triggers. The hypothesis: stochastic K/D crossovers become reliable when the baseline itself doesn't oscillate. T3 provides this stability while maintaining responsiveness. If stochastic_reversal failed due to noisy baseline (not broken oscillator logic), then T3 fixes the root cause.

variants.name: t3_stoch_momentum_flip_8to1
variants.description: T3 smooth baseline + Stochastic K/D crossover. Tests if T3 fixes stochastic_reversal's whipsaw problem. 8:1 R:R.
exit_rules: Exit on opposite Stochastic crossover signal.
Exit on max bars in trade threshold.
risk_rules: Risk note: ATR-based stop and TP for deterministic execution.
Risk note: risk per trade target is 1%.
8:1 R:R proven sweet spot across all ACCEPTs.

components.notes: Triple-smoothed EMA — reduces whipsaw that killed stochastic_reversal with plain EMA
K/D crossover for momentum flip detection

advisory_context.rationale: Resurrection test — stochastic_reversal was declared dead with EMA baseline. T3 triple-smoothing may fix the root cause (noisy baseline) rather than broken oscillator logic. If this works, it rehabilitates the stochastic family.
